_comment: Special-element annotation overlay for Book I Episode 1. One concordance entry: page 18 line 36 ('F pointing down, F pointing up') — a pair of F-variant sigla embedded as initial letters of 'face to face'. Source: Hart Concordance Symbols page; McHugh (1976) p.136; 1939 Faber & Faber edition.
source: Hart Concordance Symbols page ('18.36: F pointing down, F pointing up'); McHugh (1976) p.136; 1939 edition text: 'Upwap and dump em, [F-down]ace to [F-up]ace! When a'
encoding_note: See data/annotations/README.md for the full sigla Unicode reference table.

Ⅎ.note: McHugh (1976) p.136: 'laterally inverted pairs of Fs to be encountered at 018.36, 121.03, 07 and 266.22'. The pair consists of an F and its mirror image; both are represented here by U+2132 since no Unicode distinguishes the two orientations. In the 1939 text they are the initial letters of 'face to face' — '[F-down]ace to [F-up]ace'. Both stripped without leaving any gap or space marker; 'ace to ace' is what survives in the corpus.

annotations.note: Concordance 018.36: 'F pointing down'. Initial letter of first 'face' in '[F-down]ace to [F-up]ace'. 1939 text: 'Upwap and dump em, [F-down]ace to [F-up]ace! When a part so ptee'. Corpus has 'dump em, ace to ace!' — both F characters stripped with no space gap. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 018.36.

Concordance 018.36: 'F pointing up'. Initial letter of second 'face' in '[F-down]ace to [F-up]ace'. Both F-variants are represented by U+2132; they are distinguishable only by their position (first vs. second) within line 018.36. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 018.36.
